meta.title: Our Story | Carlo's Bakery São Paulo
meta.description: From Hoboken to São Paulo: four generations of the Valastro family, from Carlo Guastaffero to Buddy Valastro, the Cake Boss.

chapter1.headline: It all began with an Italian dream.
chapter1.subhead: Hoboken, New Jersey, 1910. A baker named Carlo Guastaffero opens the doors of a small bakery at 95 Washington Street. One hundred and fifteen years later, his recipe crosses the ocean.

chapter2.heading: Carlo.
chapter2.imageAlt: Historical portrait of Carlo Guastaffero, founder of the Hoboken bakery
chapter2.caption: Carlo Guastaffero, founder. Hoboken, 1910.
paragraphs: Carlo Guastaffero arrived in the United States from Altamura, in southern Italy, carrying his family's recipes and the determination of someone who crosses an ocean in search of a new life.

In the heart of Hoboken, New Jersey, he opened a small bakery that quickly became a gathering place for the Italian immigrant community. Cannoli, puff pastry, amaretti cookies — all made as in the old country.
chapter2.quote: Carlo used to say: "The pastry shop is the soul of a family."

chapter3.heading: A lineage of masters.
chapter3.subhead: From Carlo to Buddy, each generation added a chapter to this story.

e1910.title: Carlo opens the bakery
e1910.description: At 95 Washington Street in Hoboken, Carlo's Bake Shop is born.

e1964.title: Bartolo takes over
e1964.description: Bartolo Valastro Sr., future Buddy's stepfather, buys the bakery and renames it Carlo's Bakery.

e1990.title: Buddy learns from his father
e1990.description: At age 11, Buddy Valastro Jr. is already working beside his father, learning every recipe.

e1994.title: Buddy takes over at 17
e1994.description: After his father's death, Buddy takes over the bakery at just 17. He saves the business from bankruptcy.

e2009.title: Cake Boss is born
e2009.description: TLC premieres the reality show that would make Buddy a global star. More than 200 episodes across 12 seasons.

e2015.title: Buddy in Brazil
e2015.description: The first Carlo's Bakery outside the United States opens in São Paulo. Still the only one in Brazil today.

eToday.title: Living tradition
eToday.description: Four generations later, Carlo's is still family. And still Italian.

chapter4.heading: Buddy.
chapter4.imageAlt: Editorial portrait of Buddy Valastro, pastry chef and Cake Boss star
paragraphs: At 17, Buddy inherited a bakery on the brink of bankruptcy and the mission to honor his father's legacy. Within a decade, he turned Carlo's Bakery into a world reference in artisan pastry.

In 2009, TLC premiered "Cake Boss," the reality show that captured daily life at the bakery. The series became a global phenomenon, aired in more than 100 countries. Buddy became the world's most famous pastry chef.
But for him, the story was never about fame. It was always about family.
chapter4.quote: "Family means everything. Everything you do, you do for your family."

chapter6.heading: From Hoboken to São Paulo.
chapter6.subhead: In 2015, after decades of requests from Brazilian fans, Buddy Valastro chose São Paulo to open the first (and only) Carlo's Bakery outside the United States. It wasn't chance. It was destiny.

chapter7.heading: Carlo's in Brazil.
paragraphs: For ten years, we have kept the Valastro family tradition alive in São Paulo. Every cannoli, every lobster tail, every sculptural cake carries the same soul Carlo Guastaffero put into his bakery in 1910.
More than a franchise, we are guardians of a legacy. And the only address where, outside the United States, you can taste the real Carlo's.

galleryAlt.0: Carlo's Bakery storefront display in São Paulo

chapter8.headline: One hundred and fifteen years later, it's still family.
chapter8.subhead: Every visit to Carlo's is another chapter in this story. Be part of it.
